A. Audit Committee of the Board

hierarchy.title: भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक
hierarchy.text:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक

RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/DOR/2025-26/249 DOR.GOV.REC.No.168/29.67.001/2025-26 November 28, 2025
Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Governance) Directions

children.title: Table of Contents
children.text: Table of Contents

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Role of Nominee Directors and Non-Official Directors .................................. 4
hierarchy.text: Chapter II - Role of Nominee Directors and Non-Official Directors .................................. 4

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act,
1949, and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve Bank of India ('RBI') in this
regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest so
to do, hereby issues the Directions hereinafter specified.
Chapter-I Preliminary

children.title: A. Short title and commencement
children.text:
A. Short title and commencement
1.
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural
Banks - Governance) Directions, 2025.
2.
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.

children.title: B. Applicability
children.text:
B. Applicability
3.
These Directions shall be applicable to Regional Rural Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'RRBs' and individually as an 'RRB') as defined under
Clause (ja) of Section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, to the extent they
are not inconsistent with the statutory provisions or instructions issued
thereunder.

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text:
C. Definitions
4.
In these directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Chairman', in relation to an RRB, means the individual appointed or re-appointed
under sub-section (1) of section 11 of The Regional Rural Banks Act, 1976, as
the Chairman of that bank.
(2) 'Commercial Banks' means Public Sector Banks, Private Sector Banks, Regional
Rural Banks and Foreign Banks.
(3) 'Director', in relation to an RRB, means a member of the Board of that RRB.
(4) 'Sponsor Bank' means the bank defined under Clause (g) of Section 2 of the
Regional Rural Banks Act, 1976.
5.
All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949, the Regional Rural Banks Act, 1976, or any

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
statutory modification or re-enactment thereto, or Glossary of Terms published
by RBI or as used in common or commercial parlance, as the case may be.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-II Role of Nominee Directors and Non-Official Directors
6.
The directors on the Board of an RRB shall ensure adherence to the following
illustrative, but not exhaustive, list of DO'S and DONT'S:
(1) DO'S: A board member shall

children.title: (i) attend Board meetings regularly and effectively;
children.text: (i) attend Board meetings regularly and effectively;

children.title: Explanation: Under Section 13(1)(b) of the RRBs Act 1976, the seat of a
children.text:
Explanation: Under Section 13(1)(b) of the RRBs Act 1976, the seat of a
nominee director who remains absent without leave of the Board for
more than 3 consecutive meetings shall become vacant.

children.title: (ii) study the Board papers, follow up and seek the assistance of the
children.text:
(ii) study the Board papers, follow up and seek the assistance of the
Chairman for eliciting any information;

children.title: (iii) ask the Chairman to furnish the board papers and follow up reports on a
children.text:
(iii) ask the Chairman to furnish the board papers and follow up reports on a
definite time schedule;

children.title: (iv) be involved in the follow up of the matter of formulation of general policy
children.text:
(iv) be involved in the follow up of the matter of formulation of general policy
and ensure performance;

children.title: (v) be familiar with the objectives of the bank and policies laid down by GoI
children.text:
(v) be familiar with the objectives of the bank and policies laid down by GoI
and RBI, and give constructive ideas for better management;

children.title: (vi) work as a team member without any prejudice;
children.text: (vi) work as a team member without any prejudice;

children.title: (vii) try to give as much of their wisdom, guidance and knowledge as
children.text:
(vii) try to give as much of their wisdom, guidance and knowledge as
possible;

children.title: (viii) analyse the trends of economy and assist in discharge of the
children.text:
(viii) analyse the trends of economy and assist in discharge of the
responsibility; and

children.title: (ix) disclose their interest whenever any advance for any proposal in which
children.text:
(ix) disclose their interest whenever any advance for any proposal in which
they are directly or indirectly interested,
(2) DONT'S: A board member shall not

children.title: (i) participate in meetings in which any advance for any proposal in which
children.text:
(i) participate in meetings in which any advance for any proposal in which
they are directly or indirectly interested is discussed;

children.title: (ii) send any instructions to any individual officer or give direction to
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iii) involve in any administrative matters;
children.text: (iii) involve in any administrative matters;

children.title: (iv) interfere in the day to day functioning of the bank;
children.text: (iv) interfere in the day to day functioning of the bank;

children.title: (v) insist on getting facilities from an individual branch manager;
children.text: (v) insist on getting facilities from an individual branch manager;

children.title: (vi) involve in the day to day business of management's functions;
children.text: (vi) involve in the day to day business of management's functions;

children.title: (vii) reveal any information of the bank to anyone; and
children.text: (vii) reveal any information of the bank to anyone; and

children.title: (viii) encourage an individual officer from approaching them in any matter.
children.text: (viii) encourage an individual officer from approaching them in any matter.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-III Board Meeting Procedures
7.
The quorum of a Board meeting shall be in accordance with the rule 8 of
meetings of Board Rules notified by Government from time to time, with the
modification that if there is no quorum in the adjourned meeting of the Board, the
Chairman of RRB should give fresh notice for a fresh meeting.
8.
While outsiders including officials of the Sponsor Bank who are not Directors are
not allowed to attend meeting of the Board of an RRB to observe secrecy relating
to the affairs of the RRB's constituents, a senior official of the RRB may attend
the Board meeting to provide secretarial assistance to the Chairman. They,
however, cannot participate in the deliberations or discussions of the Board
Meetings.
9.
A model agenda for meetings of the Board of an RRB is given below:
Sr.
No.
Agenda Item Nature of
items (Statutory/
Non- statutory) 1) Minutes and Action Taken Report

i.
 Confirmation of the minutes of the previous Board meeting

ii.
 Actions taken on the decisions taken during the previous
Board meetings
2) Policies & Review of policies Statutory i.
 New policy for approval and existing policies for renewal

ii.
 Important circulars issued by RBI/ NABARD/ Government/
Sponsor Bank for adoption in toto or with suitable modifications as per the needs of the bank
iii.
 Review of important/ key policies/ Matrix of policies

iv.
 "RRBs in Focus" -- Monitorable Action Plan

3) Review of financial performance of;

i.
 Review of status of Share capital, Reserve & Surplus

ii.

improving CASA deposits, status of insurance under DICGC, interest rate under various deposits
iii.

created for borrowings, default in repayment, if any
iv.

Creditors/lnter-branch Adjustment A/c especially with Iong pending dues, provision made

items (Statutory/

guarantee/ letter of credit, amount transferred to DEAF, etc.
vii.

viii.

KCC, Agri term loan, MSME, SHG/ JLG, jewel loans, housing, retail, etc. - Review of big borrowers, Top 20/ 50 loan accounts
-- Review of high yielding advances sanctioned by the bank - Adherence to exposure norms prescribed by RBI/ NABARD - Enrolment as Member Lending Institution of different Credit Guarantee Funds under CGTMSE/ NCGTC -- Status of membership of Credit Information Companies
ix.

investment, delegation of powers, procedures, empanelment of brokers, if any -- SLR & Non-SLR investment, stop loss limit, exposure norms, status of Non-Performing Investments & provisions there against, disclosures, reporting
x.

depreciation, purchase/disposal of movable/ immovable assets, etc.
xi.
 Other Assets -- Status of interest receivable, Review of
Sundry Debtors/ Suspense Account and provision made against loss
4) Financial statements


Annual Financial statements with Auditor's Report Review
5) Profit & Loss Position

i.
 Income & Expenditure (with a view of maximising operating
profit, net profit, reduction of expenditure)
ii.

iii.

iv.
 Non-fund business activities -- Undertaking of cross selling
insurance business as an agent
v.
 PSLC traded by bank

vi.
 Review of branches/ loss making branches -- Review of
discussions held during Branch Manager's review meetings
vii.
 Status of market share of the bank's business - Review

6) Review of performance of NPAs and Standard Assets Statutory i.
Review of top 50 Standard Borrower Accounts

ii.
Review of top 50 NPA Accounts

iii.
Status of Gross NPAs

iv.
Position of SMAs

v.
Movement of NPAs

vi.
Provisions for standard assets and NPAs

Agenda Item Nature of
items (Statutory/
Non- statutory) vii.
Provision Coverage Ratio

viii.
Status of time-barred loans/ documents

ix.
Legal action cases -- suit filed/ arbitration cases, OTS, Write-

7) Performance Review of developmental areas

i.
 Annual Credit Plan -- Region-wise review

ii.

various categories
iii.
 Sustainable Viability Plan -- Target vis-a-vis achievement

iv.
 Credit to Deposit Ratio

v.

(a) KCC coverage and initiatives -- Interest Subvention/ Subsidy
scheme

children.title: (b) Crop Insurance scheme - coverage
children.text:
(b) Crop Insurance scheme - coverage

(c) Agriculture term loan and initiatives

(d) MSME loan and initiatives

(e) SHG/ JLG schemes - initiatives

(i) PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana

children.title: (m) Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana
children.text:
(m) Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana

(n) Other Flagship programmes of Gol - Pradhan Mantri Awas
Yojana, National Livelihood Mission-Ajeevika, Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry, Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana, PM Vishwakarma Scheme, Prime Minister Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprise

children.title: (o) Any other scheme
children.text:
(o) Any other scheme

8) Financial inclusion initiatives

i.
 Status of banking outlets opened including new licenses,
since previous meeting
ii.
 Status of Branch expansion iii.
 Performance review of Business Correspondents/ Corporate
BC iv.
 Status of Financial Literacy - Conduct of programmes/
campaigns
9) Technology Initiatives

i.
 Budget for CBS/CBS plus initiatives and utilization

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Agenda Item Nature of
items (Statutory/
Non- statutory) ii.
 Review of onboarding of 33 CBS plus services and usage by
customers (as stipulated by DFS, Gol)
iii.
 Functioning of ATMs and mobile/ internet banking

iv.
 Association of Fin-techs/ Agri-techs in promotion of
technology, if any
10) Status of compliance to important provisions of BR Act 1949/RBI Act 1934/RRB Act 1976/Other Acts/Rules
Statutory
applicable to RRBs
i.
 Compliance to provisions of Section 19, 20, 21, 23, 24, 26,
26A(DEAF), 27, 29, 31 & 35A of B. R. Act, 1949 Compliance to provisions of Section 42(1), 42(6)(a)(i) and 42(6)(a)(ii) of RBI Act, 1934
ii.
 Submission of statutory returns (like CTR, STR, CCR, cyber
security related VICS, Matrix of Policies, KYC/AML/CFT, fraud related VINFRA, etc.) to RBI/ FIU-INDIA (Gol)/ NABARD
iii.
 KYC/ AML/ CFT guidelines

iv.
 NABARD Inspection -- Observations and compliance

v.
 Penalties levied on the bank by RBI, if any

vi.
 Status of complaints/ Grievance Redressal Mechanism

vii.
 Compliance in respect of outstanding observations of the
Board
11) Governance System Statutory i.
 Calendar of Board meeting & Agenda items to be placed in
the meeting
ii.
 Appointment/ Change/ Leave /Absence of Directors

iii.
 Details of absence of Chairman from HQ

iv.
 Constitution and Performance of various board level
committee and other important committee like Audit Committee, Risk Management Committee, Asset Liability Management Committee, Investment Committee, Committee for monitoring of frauds, IT related Committees, Business Advisory Committee in RRBs in Focus, Customer Services Committee
v.
 Chairman General Manager's branch visits

vi.
 Payment of Sitting fee to Directors and payment of TA, etc. to
Chairman
vii.
 Appeals of the staff against punishment order passed by the
Chairman and Disciplinary authority
viii.
 Details of special rates approved by Chairman/ Bank for
special/ bulk deposits, if any
ix.
 Tie-up arrangements of bank with different companies/
agencies/ vendors
x.
 Delegation of discretionary power for Capital and Revenue
expenditure by sanctioning authority
xi.
 Credit facilities sanctioned under the powers of Chairman/
Head Office Level Credit Committee

children.title: <image>

Agenda Item Nature of
items (Statutory/
Non- statutory) xii.
 Prudential write-off approved by Chairman/ Committee

12) Internal Checks & Control System Statutory i.
Observations of Audit Committee and compliance

ii.
Observations of IS Audit and compliance

iii.

iv.
Review of frauds/ embezzlements/ thefts/ burglaries/
robberies

ii.

iii.

14) Human Resource Management & Capacity building

Recruitment

15) Inspection & Audit Statutory i.
Status of Branch inspection

iii.
Branch rating

iv.
Appointment of Statutory Auditors, Concurrent Auditors,

Observations of Statutory Auditors (LFAR), Concurrent

Agenda Item Nature of
items (Statutory/
Non- statutory) Auditors, Internal Auditors, and compliance thereto

children.title: Explanation: The list of items indicated in the above model agenda are indicative
children.text:
Explanation: The list of items indicated in the above model agenda are indicative
in nature and an RRB may make suitable changes as it deems fit keeping in view
the best corporate governance standards.

children.title: 10. The fees and allowances payable to the Non-Official Directors on the Board of
children.text:
10. The fees and allowances payable to the Non-Official Directors on the Board of
an RRB shall be as as notified by GoI from time to time.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-IV Committees of the Board

children.title: A. Audit Committee of the Board
children.text: A. Audit Committee of the Board

children.title: 11. An RRB may set up an independent Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) for
children.text:
11. An RRB may set up an independent Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) for
overseeing and providing direction to its internal audit, inspection machinery,
Statutory (external) auditors and other executives. It may have direct, unfettered
and independent access to management, internal audit, and statutory auditors,
with whom it needs to communicate in confidence, independent of each other.

children.title: 12. The ACB of an RRB may consist of three / four directors with one or more of such
children.text:
12. The ACB of an RRB may consist of three / four directors with one or more of such
directors having experience in management, finance, accountancy, audit
systems, etc.
Provided that,

children.title: (i) in case no director/s are available in the Board to function in the ACB
children.text:
(i) in case no director/s are available in the Board to function in the ACB
with adequate background in these areas, the Board may consider co-
opting a Chartered Accountant or other professionally qualified person in
the ACB.

children.title: (ii) the Chairman of the RRB may not be a member of the ACB;
children.text: (ii) the Chairman of the RRB may not be a member of the ACB;

children.title: (iii) the NABARD's Nominee Director may be inducted into the ACB.
children.text: (iii) the NABARD's Nominee Director may be inducted into the ACB.

children.title: (iv) the normal convention of choosing the senior-most Director of the Board
children.text:
(iv) the normal convention of choosing the senior-most Director of the Board
as the Chairman of the ACB may be adopted and this could be done by
rotation annually, if considered necessary, due to the changes in the
incumbencies of the Directors on the Board;

children.title: (v) while the representative of Head Office of an RRB, who is associated
children.text:
(v) while the representative of Head Office of an RRB, who is associated
with the conduct of the ACB meetings, may ideally be in a position to
furnish clarifications required by the Committee in respect of issues
pertaining to any of the Regions, the presence of concerned Regional
Managers may be enlisted, if warranted, solely for the purpose of eliciting
their views on critical issues pertaining to their respective Region.

children.title: 13. The Board of Directors may ensure that Audit Committee members are
children.text:
13. The Board of Directors may ensure that Audit Committee members are
independent, understand banking and financial matters and have the
characteristics to serve as effective Audit Committee members. For this purpose,
they may consider whether the members of the ACB:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) possess a general understanding of the major operational, liquidity,
children.text:
(i) possess a general understanding of the major operational, liquidity,
credit, interest rates and reputational risks faced by the RRB;

children.title: (ii) have a broad awareness of the RRB's statutory and other obligations as
children.text:
(ii) have a broad awareness of the RRB's statutory and other obligations as
well as various business-related operations and their financial reporting;

children.title: (iii) have the ability to formulate and ask probing questions about the RRB's
(iv) possess the required competence and will to discuss with auditors and
(v) insist unrelentingly on legal compliance, accuracy in financial reporting,
children.text:
(v) insist unrelentingly on legal compliance, accuracy in financial reporting,
and truthfulness as the foremost objective of the RRB.

children.title: 14. The ACB may meet at least four times annually, or more frequently as
children.text:
14. The ACB may meet at least four times annually, or more frequently as
circumstances dictate. In order to obviate the problem arising out of lack of
quorum in Audit Committee meetings, the RRB may arrange to fix these
meetings by either pre-fixing or suffixing the same to their Board Meetings in
normal circumstances. Conducting meetings of the ACB either in the forenoon or
afternoon of the same day as the Board meetings may also aid in economising
the expenditure.

children.title: 15. The ACB may also meet, at least half-yearly, with management, the chief of the
children.text:
15. The ACB may also meet, at least half-yearly, with management, the chief of the
statutory audit / inspection team and the department of accounts in separate
sessions to discuss any matters that the Committee or each of these groups
believe should be discussed privately.

children.title: 16. The ACB may oversee the internal controls and financial reporting process of the
children.text:
16. The ACB may oversee the internal controls and financial reporting process of the
RRB as implemented by management, identification and quantification of various
financial risks faced by the RRB and controls related to that process, and
ensuring a fair and transparent financial reporting process. This includes
oversight responsibility for financial reporting risks (including fraud risks) and
controls related to those risks.

children.title: 17. The ACB may assist the Board of Directors in fulfilling its oversight
children.text:
17. The ACB may assist the Board of Directors in fulfilling its oversight
responsibilities by reviewing the RRB's:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) financial reports and other financial information provided by the RRB to
children.text:
(i) financial reports and other financial information provided by the RRB to
any statutory body or the public;

children.title: (ii) internal controls regarding funds, accounting, legal compliance, ethics
children.text:
(ii) internal controls regarding funds, accounting, legal compliance, ethics
that management and the Board have established; and,

children.title: (iii) auditing, accounting and financial reporting processes.
children.text: (iii) auditing, accounting and financial reporting processes.

children.title: 18. For the above purpose, an ACB may perform, inter-alia, the following functions:
children.text: 18. For the above purpose, an ACB may perform, inter-alia, the following functions:

children.title: (i) provide an open channel of communication among the external /
(ii) serve as an effective and objective mechanism to monitor the RRB's
children.text:
(ii) serve as an effective and objective mechanism to monitor the RRB's
financial reporting process and internal control system;

children.title: (iii) ensure expeditious closing of books of accounts as on March 31 every
children.text:
(iii) ensure expeditious closing of books of accounts as on March 31 every
year with a view to prepare the Balance-Sheet and Profit & Loss Account
within the time limit prescribed in the BR Act, 1949 and review the Annual
Report with financial management and the accounts department prior to
its release. The Chairman of the Audit Committee may represent the
ACB for purposes of this review;

children.title: (iv) review the RRB's annual financial statements and any reports or other
children.text:
(iv) review the RRB's annual financial statements and any reports or other
financial information submitted to any statutory body, or the public,
including any certification, report, opinion, or review rendered by the
auditors; and ensure that management has a proper review system to
ensure such dissemination satisfies legal requirements;

children.title: (v) review any significant disagreement between the management and the
(vi) review the integrity of the RRB's financial reporting processes
children.text:
(vi) review the integrity of the RRB's financial reporting processes
(especially, the correctness of NPAs and provisioning for impaired
assets as per RBI norms) and monitor the "key risks and controls"
thereof;

children.title: (vii) evaluate the accounts department's judgments about the quality and
children.text:
(vii) evaluate the accounts department's judgments about the quality and
appropriateness of the RRB's accounting principles as applied in its
financial reporting. The Committee may also ensure a periodic review of
the RRB's accounting policies, systems, and controls to enhance

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
transparency in financial reporting and ensure adequacy of controls in
addressing current and emerging risks faced by the RRB;

children.title: (viii) consider and approve, if appropriate, major changes to the RRB's
children.text:
(viii) consider and approve, if appropriate, major changes to the RRB's
auditing principles and practices as suggested by the accounts
department, management, or the internal Audit Department / External
Auditors / NABARD Inspection Team;

children.title: (ix) review with the accounts department, the internal audit department and
children.text:
(ix) review with the accounts department, the internal audit department and
management the extent to which changes or improvements in financial
or accounting practices, as approved by the Audit Committee, have been
implemented. This review may be conducted at an appropriate time
subsequent to implementation of changes or improvements, as decided
by the ACB.

children.title: (x) recommend to the Board of Directors the selection of the Internal
children.text:
(x) recommend to the Board of Directors the selection of the Internal
Auditor(s) considering their independence and effectiveness;

children.title: (xi) review the activities, organizational structure, and qualifications of the
children.text:
(xi) review the activities, organizational structure, and qualifications of the
internal audit department; including a review of different types of
inspections / audits (Internal Inspection, Internal Audit, Concurrent Audit,
etc.) conducted within the RRB, their periodicity and scheduling,
coverage and quality to ensure an effective internal audit function;

children.title: (xii) evaluate the competence and ensure independence of the internal and
children.text:
(xii) evaluate the competence and ensure independence of the internal and
external auditors;

children.title: (xiii) review the performance of the internal auditor / accounts officer;
children.text: (xiii) review the performance of the internal auditor / accounts officer;

children.title: (xiv) periodically consult with the senior accountant / accounts officer, without
children.text:
(xiv) periodically consult with the senior accountant / accounts officer, without
the presence of management, about internal controls and the fullness
and accuracy of the organization's financial statements.

children.title: (xv) establish regular and separate systems of reporting to the Audit
children.text:
(xv) establish regular and separate systems of reporting to the Audit
Committee by the management, the accounts department and the
internal auditors regarding any significant judgments made in
management's preparation of the financial statements and the view of
each as to the appropriateness of such judgments;

children.title: (xvi) review and appraise the audits of the supervisor and RRB's external /
children.text:
(xvi) review and appraise the audits of the supervisor and RRB's external /
departmental auditors, including Half-yearly Review report of the
Statutory Auditors, and internal audit department. The Committee may
specifically examine any omission on the part of internal inspecting

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
officials or concurrent auditors to detect serious irregularities that come
to light at a later stage;

children.title: (xvii) review the regular internal reports to management prepared by the
children.text:
(xvii) review the regular internal reports to management prepared by the
internal audit department and management's response thereto;

children.title: (xviii) review and approve the compliances furnished by the RRB in respect of
(xix) following completion of the annual audit, review separately with the
children.text:
(xix) following completion of the annual audit, review separately with the
management, the accounts department and the internal audit
department any significant difficulties encountered during the course of
the audit, including any restrictions on the scope of work or access to
required information.

children.title: (xx) review of adoption of measures for improvement in controls as
children.text:
(xx) review of adoption of measures for improvement in controls as
suggested by internal/external auditors/NABARD's inspection team, with
specific emphasis on the follow-up action on Internal Inspection and
Internal Audit Reports, particularly in respect of "unsatisfactory"
branches and large branches. The Committee may also review the
follow-up on concurrent audit observations. If measures are not adopted,
compelling reasons may be in place. The ACB may also fix accountability
for unsatisfactory compliance of inspection / audit reports, delay in
compliance and non-rectification of deficiencies;

children.title: (xxi) establish, review and update periodically a Code of Ethical Conduct and
children.text:
(xxi) establish, review and update periodically a Code of Ethical Conduct and
ensure that management has established a system to enforce, monitor
and review this Code.

children.title: (xxii) review, with the organization's counsel, any legal matter that could have
children.text:
(xxii) review, with the organization's counsel, any legal matter that could have
a significant impact on the RRB's financial statements and legal
compliance matters including corporate securities trading policies.

children.title: (xxiii) certification of physical possession of securities as reported to the RBI
children.text:
(xxiii) certification of physical possession of securities as reported to the RBI
every quarter by Internal Auditors or Concurrent Auditors;

children.title: (xxiv) examine reasons for defaults in payment to the depositors and creditors
children.text:
(xxiv) examine reasons for defaults in payment to the depositors and creditors
whenever there are any cases of default in payments;

children.title: (xxv) evaluate information security audit mechanism in the RRB with particular
children.text:
(xxv) evaluate information security audit mechanism in the RRB with particular
reference to Disaster Recovery System and Business Continuity

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Planning;

children.title: (xxvi) periodic review of the progress in reconciliation of entries in inter-bank
children.text:
(xxvi) periodic review of the progress in reconciliation of entries in inter-bank
and Inter-branch accounts and reconciliation of bank accounts;

children.title: (xxvii) review of frauds and action taken to recover the amount involved, to
children.text:
(xxvii) review of frauds and action taken to recover the amount involved, to
avoid recurrence of such frauds in future;

children.title: (xxviii) ensure adherence to KYC & PMLA guidelines issued by the RBI /
(xxix) review RRB's compliance with statutory provisions with particular
children.text:
(xxix) review RRB's compliance with statutory provisions with particular
reference to Sections 24, 29, 31, 35A of Banking Regulation Act 1949,
and Section 42(1), 42(6)(a)(i) & (ii) of RBI Act, 1934; and

children.title: (xxx) any other activities consistent with these Directions, the instructions /
children.text:
(xxx) any other activities consistent with these Directions, the instructions /
guidelines issued by the RBI, NABARD or Government of India, from
time to time, and the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 etc., as the
Committee or the Board deems necessary or appropriate.
Provided that, the ACB may not be overloaded with other activities or else, the
Committee may lose sight of its major objectives or perform its duties
superficially. Further, the above list is illustrative and not exhaustive. The bank
may firm up its detailed policy keeping in view these broad guidelines and
frameworks.

children.title: 19. While an RRB may, with the approval of its Board or the ACB, decide on the
children.text:
19. While an RRB may, with the approval of its Board or the ACB, decide on the
periodicity of review of various issues and accordingly, draw an annual Calendar
of Reviews to be put up to the ACB, they may adopt the following periodicity in
respect of the undernoted items:

children.title: (i) Quarterly
children.text: (i) Quarterly

children.title: (a) Inter-branch reconciliation,
children.text: (a) Inter-branch reconciliation,

children.title: (b) unreconciled long outstanding entries in inter-branch and inter-
children.text:
(b) unreconciled long outstanding entries in inter-branch and inter-
bank accounts,

children.title: (c) Performance of Audit / Inspection Department,
children.text: (c) Performance of Audit / Inspection Department,

children.title: (d) Inspection reports of poorly rated branches - Progress in
children.text:
(d) Inspection reports of poorly rated branches - Progress in
rectification of deficiencies,

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (e) Position of house-keeping (i.e., balancing of books, clearing
children.text:
(e) Position of house-keeping (i.e., balancing of books, clearing
differences, etc.),

children.title: (f) frauds
children.text: (f) frauds

children.title: (ii) Half-yearly
children.text: (ii) Half-yearly

children.title: (a) compliance with the statutory audit / concurrent audit /
children.text:
(a) compliance with the statutory audit / concurrent audit /
Management Audit / NABARD Inspection reports,

children.title: (b) omissions on the part of Internal Inspecting Officials to detect
children.text:
(b) omissions on the part of Internal Inspecting Officials to detect
serious irregularities,

children.title: (c) periodic review of the accounting policies / systems in the RRB
children.text:
(c) periodic review of the accounting policies / systems in the RRB
with a view to ensuring greater transparency in the RRB's
accounts and adequacy of accounting controls, and

children.title: (d) Review of Loss Assets with balance of ₹1 lakh and above for
children.text:
(d) Review of Loss Assets with balance of ₹1 lakh and above for
more than two years and where legal action has not been
initiated.

children.title: B. Risk Management Committee
children.text: B. Risk Management Committee

children.title: 20. Risk Management Committee (RMC) will be a Board level sub-committee
children.text:
20. Risk Management Committee (RMC) will be a Board level sub-committee
including Chairman and heads of Credit, Market and Operational Risk
Management Committees. Since the volume, size and nature of operations of
RRBs are small as compared to the commercial banks, they may consider having
a small Credit and Operational Risk Management Cell immediately, to begin with.
Based on the quantum of investment portfolio, the Boards of the respective RRBs
can decide to have a separate Market Risk Committee at a later stage.

children.title: 21. The RMC will devise the policy and strategy for integrated risk management
children.text:
21. The RMC will devise the policy and strategy for integrated risk management
containing various risk exposures of the bank including the credit risk. For this
purpose, RMC may effectively coordinate between Credit Risk Management
Committee (CRMC), Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO) and other
risk committees of the bank, if any.

children.title: 22. The Board of the RRB may, therefore, ensure that the independence of the RMC
children.text:
22. The Board of the RRB may, therefore, ensure that the independence of the RMC
is not compromised at any cost. In the event of the Board not accepting any
recommendation of this committee, systems may be put in place to spell out the
rationale for such an action and may be properly documented. This document

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
may be made available to the internal and external auditors for their scrutiny and
comments.

children.title: 23. The credit risk strategy and policies adopted by RMC may be effectively
children.text:
23. The credit risk strategy and policies adopted by RMC may be effectively
communicated throughout the organization.

children.title: 24. An RRB may, at its discretion, constitute sub-committees and support
children.text:
24. An RRB may, at its discretion, constitute sub-committees and support
committees to assist the RMC.

children.title: 25. The RMC of an RRB may discuss the following as part of its meeting agenda:
children.text: 25. The RMC of an RRB may discuss the following as part of its meeting agenda:

children.title: (i) identification of inherent business risks in various activities undertaken
children.text:
(i) identification of inherent business risks in various activities undertaken
by the RRB;

children.title: (ii) risk measurement in terms of both uncertainty and its potential adverse
children.text:
(ii) risk measurement in terms of both uncertainty and its potential adverse
impact on profitability;

children.title: (iii) evaluation of effectiveness of the control systems for monitoring the
children.text:
(iii) evaluation of effectiveness of the control systems for monitoring the
inherent risks of the business activities (control risk);

children.title: (iv) establish proper system of Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity
children.text:
(iv) establish proper system of Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity
Planning;

children.title: (v) drawing up a risk-matrix for taking into account both the factors viz.,
children.text:
(v) drawing up a risk-matrix for taking into account both the factors viz.,
inherent business risks and control risks;

children.title: (vi) fixing parameters for classification of high, medium and low risks;
children.text: (vi) fixing parameters for classification of high, medium and low risks;

children.title: (vii) risk measurement methodology by using previous internal audit reports
children.text:
(vii) risk measurement methodology by using previous internal audit reports
and compliance;

children.title: (viii) suggest changes in loan policy and / or change prudential exposure
children.text:
(viii) suggest changes in loan policy and / or change prudential exposure
norms;

children.title: (ix) oversee significant changes in management / key personnel, to manage
children.text:
(ix) oversee significant changes in management / key personnel, to manage
'operational risk';

children.title: (x) scrutiny of Inspection report of NABARD and reports of external auditors
children.text:
(x) scrutiny of Inspection report of NABARD and reports of external auditors
and suggest suitable risk mitigation measures; and

children.title: (xi) risk focused internal audit.
children.text: (xi) risk focused internal audit.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 26. The agendas suggested for each of the committees above are illustrative and
children.text:
26. The agendas suggested for each of the committees above are illustrative and
not exhaustive, and an RRB may include additional items based on local
conditions and emergent priorities.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-VI Repeal and other provisions

children.title: A. Repeal and saving
children.text: A. Repeal and saving

children.title: 27. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
children.text:
27. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
guidelines relating to governance as applicable to Regional Rural Banks, stand
repealed, as communicated vide circular DOR.RRC.REC.302/33-01-010/2025-
26 dated November 28, 2025. The Directions, instructions, and guidelines
repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain
repealed.

children.title: 28. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
children.text:
28. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof. All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions. Further, the repeal of these directions,
instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:

children.title: (i) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
children.text: (i) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;

children.title: (ii) any, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any contravention
children.text:
(ii) any, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any contravention
committed thereunder;

children.title: (iii) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
children.text:
(iii) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
privilege, obligation, liability, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment as aforesaid; and
any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been repealed.

children.title: B. Application of other laws not barred
children.text: B. Application of other laws not barred

children.title: 29. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
children.text:
29. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: C. Interpretations
children.text: C. Interpretations

children.title: 30. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
children.text:
30. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
(Manoranjan Padhy) Chief General Manager
